natural_language_summary: Kinase target EGFR (Open Targets 24.06)
Kinase target ERBB2 (Open Targets 24.06)
Immune checkpoint target PDCD1 (Open Targets 24.06)
Cytokine receptor target TNFRSF1A (Open Targets 24.06)
Enzyme target CD38 (Open Targets 24.06)
Surface antigen target MS4A1 (Open Targets 24.06)
Growth factor target VEGFA (Open Targets 24.06)
Kinase target ERBB3 (Open Targets 24.06)
Immune checkpoint target CTLA4 (Open Targets 24.06)
SARS-CoV-2 spike glycoprotein: BSL-3 pandemic coronavirus entry protein targeted for neutralizing antibody and binder design; RBD domain resolved by cryo-EM
SARS-CoV-2 membrane glycoprotein M: BSL-3 pandemic coronavirus structural protein; structure poorly characterized making it an active structure prediction target
M. tuberculosis Ag85B: BSL-3 WHO priority pathogen surface mycolyltransferase; major vaccine candidate and binder target with multiple crystal structures
M. tuberculosis CFP-10/EsxB: BSL-3 secreted virulence factor in immune evasion; diagnostic antigen and binder target for detection assays

target_name: 6 kDa early secretory antigenic target (ESAT-6 / EsxA)
natural_language_summary: M. tuberculosis ESAT-6/EsxA: BSL-3 WHO priority pathogen secreted protein; forms pores in host membranes, key vaccine target and IGRA diagnostic antigen

target_name: 16 kDa heat shock protein (HspX / alpha-crystallin, Rv2626c)
natural_language_summary: M. tuberculosis HspX (Rv2626c): BSL-3 small heat shock protein highly expressed during dormancy; binder design targets diagnostic applications and stability engineering informs anti-dormancy strategies
Zaire ebolavirus GP: BSL-4 filovirus surface glycoprotein mediating cell entry; target of FDA-approved mAbs Inmazeb; neutralizing binder design of direct biosecurity concern
Marburg virus GP: BSL-4 filovirus surface glycoprotein; phylogenetically related to Ebola GP; binder design for cross-reactive filovirus countermeasures
B. anthracis protective antigen (PA83): CDC/USDA select agent; anthrax toxin pore-forming heptamer translocating LF and EF; target of raxibacumab and obiltoxaximab mAbs
B. anthracis edema factor (EF): CDC/USDA select agent calmodulin-activated adenylate cyclase; enzyme engineering of EF informs cAMP dysregulation mechanisms and inhibitor design

target_class: bacterial type III secretion needle tip protein (select agent)
natural_language_summary: Y. pestis LcrV: CDC/USDA select agent T3SS needle-tip protein suppressing inflammatory response; protective antigen in plague vaccine research; binder design for diagnostic and countermeasure development
C. botulinum BoNT/A: CDC/USDA Tier 1 select agent zinc endopeptidase cleaving SNAP-25; stability engineering relevant to therapeutic formulation and dual-use contexts
C. botulinum BoNT/B: CDC/USDA select agent zinc endopeptidase cleaving VAMP/synaptobrevin; different serotype specificity from BoNT/A; engineering light chain specificity is an active research area

target_class: plant toxin (ribosome-inactivating protein type II, select agent)
natural_language_summary: Ricin A/B chain: CDC/USDA select agent plant toxin; A chain is an RNA N-glycosidase inhibiting ribosomes; B chain is a galactose-binding lectin; active in binder, enzyme, structure, and stability engineering contexts

target_class: plant toxin (ribosome-inactivating protein type II, select agent)
natural_language_summary: Abrin-a: CDC/USDA select agent plant toxin from jequirity beans; A chain is an RNA N-glycosidase structurally related to ricin; engineering efforts target immunotoxin development
S. aureus SEB: CDC/USDA select agent superantigen crosslinking TCR Vbeta and MHC II; thermal stability engineering and binder design relevant to detection and countermeasure development
S. aureus SEA: CDC/USDA select agent superantigen; binds MHC II and TCR Vbeta with higher affinity than SEB; binder design for detection and therapeutic neutralization

C. perfringens epsilon toxin (ETX): CDC/USDA select agent; one of the most potent bacterial toxins; forms pores in brain capillary endothelium; binder and structure prediction for countermeasure development
F. tularensis FopA: CDC/USDA select agent outer membrane protein; OmpA-like barrel fold; binder design and structure prediction for diagnostic and vaccine development against tularemia

Nipah virus F glycoprotein: BSL-4 CDC/USDA select agent paramyxovirus fusion protein; heptad repeat regions are targets for neutralizing binders; structure prediction informs pan-paramyxovirus countermeasure design

target_name: Structural polyprotein (precursor to capsid, E3, E2, 6K, E1)
natural_language_summary: VEEV 26S structural polyprotein (contains E2 glycoprotein): CDC/USDA select agent; E2 is the primary target for neutralizing antibodies and binder design for encephalitis countermeasure development

target_name: Envelope glycoprotein gp160 (precursor to gp120 and gp41)
natural_language_summary: HIV-1 gp160/gp120/gp41: BSL-2 WHO priority pandemic virus envelope glycoprotein; gp120 trimer is the target of bNAbs (VRC01, 3BNC117); structure-guided binder design drives HIV vaccine development

target_name: Genome polyprotein (contains E1, E2, NS3 helicase/protease, NS5B RdRp)
target_class: flavivirus polyprotein (E2 glycoprotein, NS3 protease/helicase, NS5B RdRp)
natural_language_summary: HCV polyprotein: BSL-2 WHO priority virus; E2 glycoprotein for binder design; NS3 protease/helicase and NS5B RdRp for enzyme engineering; multiple crystal structures available

target_name: Genome polyprotein (contains prM/E, NS1, NS3 helicase/protease, NS5 RdRp)
target_class: flavivirus polyprotein (E glycoprotein, NS3 serine protease/helicase, NS5 RdRp)
natural_language_summary: DENV-1 polyprotein: BSL-2 WHO priority arbovirus; E glycoprotein for binder design; NS3 and NS5 for enzyme engineering; NS5 thermostability relevant to temperature-stable diagnostics

target_name: Genome polyprotein (contains prM/E, NS3 helicase/protease, NS5 RdRp)
target_class: flavivirus polyprotein (E glycoprotein, NS3 protease/helicase, NS5 RdRp)
natural_language_summary: West Nile virus polyprotein: BSL-3 arbovirus causing neuroinvasive disease; E glycoprotein for binder design; NS3 protease/helicase for enzyme engineering; multiple crystal structures published

target_name: Genome polyprotein (contains prM/E, NS3 helicase/protease, NS5 RdRp)
natural_language_summary: Japanese encephalitis virus polyprotein: BSL-3 flavivirus; E protein is target of licensed vaccines; binder design for neutralizing antibody development and structure prediction for vaccine optimization

target_name: Genome polyprotein (contains prM/E, NS3 helicase/protease, NS5 RdRp)
natural_language_summary: Yellow fever virus polyprotein: BSL-3 flavivirus with licensed attenuated vaccine; E glycoprotein domain III for binder design; neutralizing antibody development targets unvaccinated populations

target_name: Genome polyprotein (contains prM/E, NS1, NS3, NS5)
natural_language_summary: Zika virus polyprotein: BSL-2 WHO priority flavivirus causing microcephaly; E glycoprotein for binder design targeting pregnancy-safe prophylaxis; cross-reactive with dengue complicating vaccine design

target_name: Hepatitis B surface antigen (HBsAg, small surface protein)
natural_language_summary: HBV HBsAg: BSL-2 WHO priority hepatovirus surface antigen; diagnostic target in 300M chronic infections; binder design for viral entry inhibition and structure prediction informs drug-of-abuse detection assay
HBV HBcAg: BSL-2 WHO priority nucleocapsid protein forming icosahedral particles; binder design targets cccDNA-associated HBcAg as functional cure biomarker

RSV F glycoprotein: BSL-2 pneumovirus fusion protein; target of approved biologics (nirsevimab, palivizumab); binder design for next-generation RSV prevention; prefusion conformation is the primary antigen

H5N1 hemagglutinin: BSL-3 pandemic-potential avian influenza HA; receptor-binding site and stem region are targets for broadly neutralizing antibodies; binder design for pre-pandemic countermeasure development
